_README: Pinned baseline + ceiling for Sprint 19 perf gate. baseline_us = the median measured on dev hardware (Linux 6.6 / WSL2, NVMe, dev laptop) at smoke config (--warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 2 --sample-size 20). ceiling_us = the value above which CI flags a regression. Entries marked `informational: true` are reported but never counted toward the gate — they conflate per-call cost with iter-count due to graph-state compounding (internal design notes), so their median is sensitive to criterion's auto-tuned iter count and not a stable regression metric. Once Sprint 19 item #2 fix lands and these benches stop compounding, drop the `informational` flag and pin them as gates. Headroom on the gate-eligible cold benches is intentionally generous (~3× baseline) for phase-1 because GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest runners are slower + noisier than the dev box; the first 5-10 CI runs should produce the real CI-hardware distribution and ceilings re-pinned against CI medians + 2× variance band before flipping PERF_FAIL_ON_REGRESSION=1.
